summary: Implement M3 polity, suzerain chain, and auditable obligations model
acceptance_criteria: M3 WorldState and runtime content represent Polity records, one direct suzerain per polity, district controller references, obligation records, and obligation audit history with branded IDs and explicit serializable schemas.
Suzerain chains are acyclic and deterministic tie-breakers are specified for every traversal or ordered obligation audit.

Each obligation has amount or condition, due cadence or trigger, status/history, dispute or breach reason, and stable audit event references; the same payment or troop fulfillment cannot be double-counted across suzerains.
All polity/vassalage mutations occur inside sim-core through versioned GameCommand paths usable by both player and AI actors.

The model does not implement full diplomacy, complete war, legitimacy, formal historical content, object graph serialization, runtime SQL/ORM, server, Rust/WASM, Node filesystem IO in sim-core, or UI behavior.
required_tests: Property/invariant tests cover suzerain-chain acyclicity, stable obligation audit ordering, no duplicate fulfillment counting, and district controller validity.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-POLITY-VASSALAGE-001
notes: This task establishes the M3 polity/vassalage substrate only; it must not absorb postwar settlement, full diplomacy, or client UI work.
Keep sim-core decoupled from React, Pixi, Electron, DOM, Node IO, and real-time APIs.
Any change that would reinterpret D-012 or require a new architecture decision must stop and route to systems_architect.
